text: What is the capital of Russia?
explanation: The capital of Russia is Moscow.

text: What is the capital of France?
explanation: The capital of France is Paris.

text: Which is the largest ocean on Earth?
explanation: The largest ocean is the Pacific.

text: Which river is considered the longest in the world?
explanation: By traditional measurements the Nile is regarded as the longest river.

text: On which continent is the Sahara Desert?
explanation: The Sahara lies in North Africa.

text: How many continents are there on Earth?
explanation: It is common to count seven continents.

text: What is the capital of Italy?
explanation: The capital of Italy is Rome.

text: Which is the largest country by area?
explanation: Russia is the largest country in the world by area.

text: Which is the highest mountain in the world?
explanation: The highest peak is Mount Everest (8,849 m).

text: What is the capital of Japan?
explanation: The capital of Japan is Tokyo.

text: Which is the coldest continent?
What is the capital of Germany?
explanation: The capital of Germany is Berlin.

text: Which body of water is one of the saltiest in the world?
explanation: The Dead Sea is famous for its very high salinity.

text: On which continent does most of Russia lie?
explanation: Russia is on the continent of Eurasia.

text: What is the capital of the United Kingdom?
explanation: The capital of the UK is London.

text: Which ocean washes the east coast of the USA?
explanation: The east coast of the USA is on the Atlantic Ocean.

text: Which is the deepest lake in the world?
explanation: The deepest lake is Lake Baikal (over 1,600 m).

text: What is the capital of the United States?
explanation: The capital of the USA is Washington, D.C.

text: Which country is shaped like a boot on the map?
What is the capital of China?
explanation: The capital of China is Beijing.

text: What is the capital of Australia?
explanation: The capital of Australia is Canberra, not Sydney.

text: Which is the longest river in Europe?
explanation: The longest river in Europe is the Volga.

text: Which strait separates Europe and Africa near the town of Gibraltar?
explanation: Europe and Africa are separated here by the Strait of Gibraltar.

text: On which continent is Egypt?
explanation: Most of Egypt lies in Africa.

text: What is the capital of Canada?
explanation: The capital of Canada is Ottawa.

text: Which is the largest hot desert in the world?
explanation: The largest hot desert is the Sahara.

text: Which body of water is the largest lake on the planet by area?
explanation: The Caspian Sea is the largest lake on Earth by area.

text: What is the capital of Brazil?
explanation: The capital of Brazil is the city of Brasília.

text: Through which country does most of the Amazon River flow?
explanation: Most of the Amazon basin lies within Brazil.

text: Which mountains traditionally divide Europe and Asia?
explanation: The conventional boundary of Europe and Asia runs along the Ural Mountains.

text: What is the capital of Spain?
explanation: The capital of Spain is Madrid.

text: Which canal connects the Mediterranean and the Red Sea?
explanation: The Mediterranean and the Red Sea are linked by the Suez Canal.

text: Which is the largest island in the world?
explanation: The largest island on the planet is Greenland.

text: What is the capital of Egypt?
explanation: The capital of Egypt is Cairo.

text: On which continent is the Amazon rainforest?
explanation: The Amazon rainforest is in South America.

text: Which island nation includes the islands of Honshu and Hokkaido?
explanation: Honshu and Hokkaido are the largest islands of Japan.

text: What is the capital of Turkey?
explanation: The capital of Turkey is Ankara, not Istanbul.

text: Which is the smallest country in the world by area?
explanation: The smallest country is Vatican City.

text: On which peninsula does most of India lie?
explanation: India occupies most of the Indian subcontinent.

text: What is the capital of Greece?
explanation: The capital of Greece is Athens.

text: What is the capital of Argentina?
explanation: The capital of Argentina is Buenos Aires.

text: Which is the highest waterfall in the world?
explanation: The highest waterfall is Angel Falls in Venezuela.

text: Which country has the largest population in the world?
explanation: Since 2023 the most populous country has been India.

text: How many time zones does Russia span?
What is the capital of Norway?
explanation: The capital of Norway is Oslo.

text: Where is the deepest point of the world's oceans?
explanation: The deepest point is the Mariana Trench (about 11 km).

text: On which river does the capital of Egypt stand?
explanation: Cairo stands on the River Nile.

text: What is the de facto capital of Switzerland?
explanation: The de facto capital of Switzerland is Bern.

text: Which strait separates Russia from Alaska?
explanation: Russia and Alaska are separated by the Bering Strait.

text: Which is the largest freshwater lake by surface area?
explanation: The largest freshwater lake by area is Lake Superior in North America.

text: What is the capital of India?
explanation: The capital of India is New Delhi.

text: Which country occupies an entire continent of its own?
explanation: Australia is the only country that occupies a whole continent.

text: On which continent are the Andes, the longest mountain range on land?
explanation: The Andes run along the western edge of South America.

text: What is the capital of Poland?
explanation: The capital of Poland is Warsaw.

text: Which sea in the Atlantic has no land borders and is bounded by ocean currents?
explanation: The Sargasso Sea is bounded by ocean currents rather than land.

text: Which is the highest mountain in Africa?
explanation: The highest peak in Africa is Mount Kilimanjaro.

text: What is the capital of Sweden?
explanation: The capital of Sweden is Stockholm.

text: Which major city lies on two continents at once?
explanation: Istanbul lies in both Europe and Asia, split by the Bosphorus.

text: Which is the smallest country in South America by area?
explanation: The smallest country in mainland South America is Suriname.

text: What is the official capital of the Netherlands?
explanation: The official capital of the Netherlands is Amsterdam (the government sits in The Hague).

text: Which oceanic trench is the deepest known point in the Earth's oceans?
explanation: The Mariana Trench (Challenger Deep) reaches a depth of approximately 11,000 meters.

text: In which year did Christopher Columbus first land in the Bahamas, initiating the discovery of America?
explanation: Columbus's expedition reached the island of San Salvador on October 12, 1492.

text: Which climate type is characteristic of regions located directly along the Earth's equator?
explanation: An equatorial climate is marked by hot temperatures and heavy rainfall year-round.

text: Relative to which meridian (Greenwich) is Coordinated Universal Time (UTC) calculated?
explanation: The Greenwich Meridian passes through the Royal Observatory in Greenwich, London.

text: Through which desert, the largest hot desert in the world, does the Nile River flow?
explanation: The Nile River crosses the Sahara from south to north, emptying into the Mediterranean.

text: Which desert on Earth is the largest by area, including cold regions?
explanation: The Antarctic polar desert exceeds the Sahara in area and is technically the driest.

text: Which explorer led the first expedition in history to circumnavigate the globe (1519-1522)?
explanation: Magellan died in the Philippines, but his ship completed the voyage under Juan Sebastian Elcano.

text: What area distortion is characteristic of the classic cylindrical Mercator projection on world maps?
explanation: The Mercator projection preserves angles but severely distorts area sizes near the poles.

text: Along the borders of which lithospheric plates of the Pacific Ocean is the Ring of Fire located?
explanation: The Ring of Fire features high tectonic activity, hosting about 90% of Earth's earthquakes.

text: What climate phenomenon is characterized by abnormal warming of sea surface temperatures in the eastern Pacific?
explanation: El Nino weakens trade winds and drastically shifts weather patterns across the globe.

text: Which 1494 treaty divided newly discovered lands outside Europe between Spain and Portugal along a meridian?
explanation: The Treaty of Tordesillas set a boundary dividing spheres of influence in the Atlantic.
